story.asA: photographer with files in different formats
story.iWant: per-file conversion controls to select different target formats for each file
story.soThat: I can convert files to different formats in a single batch without running separate conversions

tasks.task: Add Format Selection Dropdown to File Cards (AC-1)
Style Format Dropdown and Convert Button (AC-1, AC-2)
Implement Batch and Individual Conversion Coexistence (AC-6)

criterion.title: Per-File Format Selection Dropdown
criterion.description: Each file card displays "Convert to..." dropdown showing only valid target formats
criterion.verification: - Each file card displays "Convert to..." dropdown - Dropdown shows only valid target formats for the source format (4 options, excludes source) - Dropdown disabled during conversion ("processing" state) - Dropdown hidden when conversion complete ("complete" state) - Default selection: First valid target format (alphabetically)

criterion.title: Individual "Convert" Button
criterion.description: Each file card has "Convert" button that converts only that file
criterion.verification: - Each file card has "Convert" button - Button enabled when file in "queued" state - Button disabled during conversion ("processing" state) - Button hidden when conversion complete ("complete" state, replaced by download) - Button text: "Convert" (no icon needed) - Clicking button starts conversion for that file only (not batch)

criterion.title: Individual File Conversion Flow
criterion.description: User can convert files in any order with proper state transitions
criterion.verification: - User selects target format from dropdown - User clicks "Convert" button - File status changes: queued to processing to complete/error - Only selected file converts (other files remain in queued state) - User can convert files in any order (file 3 before file 1, etc.) - Batch progress indicator shows per-file progress

criterion.title: Mixed Format Conversion Support
criterion.description: User can select different target formats for different files
criterion.verification: - User can select different target formats for different files - Each file converts to its selected target format - Download buttons show correct format: "Download XMP", "Download lrtemplate", etc. - Downloaded filenames reflect target format: preset_converted.xmp, style_converted.lrtemplate

criterion.title: Retry Failed Conversions
criterion.description: Files in "error" state can be retried indefinitely
criterion.verification: - Files in "error" state show "Retry" button (replaces "Convert" button) - Clicking "Retry" re-attempts conversion with same target format - Error message clears when retry starts (status to processing) - If retry succeeds status changes to complete - If retry fails error message updates with new error - Retry attempts not limited (user can retry indefinitely)

criterion.title: Batch vs Individual Conversion Coexistence
criterion.description: Batch and individual conversions work together without conflict
criterion.verification: - Batch "Convert All" button converts all queued files to same format - Individual "Convert" buttons convert single files to custom formats - User can mix: batch convert 5 files, then individually convert remaining 2 - Batch progress tracks both batch-converted and individually-converted files - No conflict: user cannot trigger batch and individual conversions simultaneously

criterion.title: Conversion Cancel for Individual Files
criterion.description: Individual file conversion can be cancelled during processing
criterion.verification: - Individual file conversion can be cancelled during "processing" state - File card shows "Cancel" button (replaces "Convert") - Clicking "Cancel" aborts conversion for that file - File status reverts to "queued" (user can convert again) - Cancelling individual file does not affect other files - No confirmation dialog for individual cancel

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: Per-file conversion controls allow users to select different target formats for each file and convert files individually or in batch mode. Supports retry for failed conversions and individual file cancellation during processing.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document (Path A)
doc.snippet: Improved conversion flow with format detection, dropdown format selection, batch and individual conversion options, instant conversion less than 100ms, download buttons, and "Start Fresh" to reset interface.

doc.title: Story 10.2: Batch File Upload
doc.snippet: Provides foundation for file upload handling with UploadManager class, file card components showing filename/format badge/size/status, and grid layout for batch files.

doc.title: Story 10.3: Progress Indicators
doc.snippet: Implements per-file status tracking (queued/processing/complete/error), updateFileStatus() function, download button functionality, and batch progress indicators for multi-file conversions.

doc.title: Story 4.3: TUI Batch Progress Display
doc.snippet: TUI implementation of batch conversion with worker pool pattern, sequential processing, progress tracking, and cancellation handling. Demonstrates batch/individual conversion coordination patterns.

file.reason: Current web interface structure - shows existing drop zone, status displays, and conversion controls that will be extended for individual file actions

file.lines: N/A - to be created in Story 10-2
file.reason: Core upload management class that will be extended to support per-file conversion, format selection dropdowns, and retry functionality
WASM conversion bridge that accepts file data and target format - will be called per-file for individual conversions with AbortSignal support

file.lines: N/A - to be created in Story 10-2
file.reason: File card component styles that will be extended with conversion section (.file-card__conversion) for format dropdown and convert button
TUI batch conversion implementation demonstrates sequential processing pattern and concurrency control that applies to web individual conversions

javascript.purpose: No external dependencies - native Web APIs only

browser-api.name: FileReader API
browser-api.purpose: Read uploaded file contents as ArrayBuffer

browser-api.name: AbortController API
browser-api.purpose: Cancel individual file conversions in progress

browser-api.name: Blob API
browser-api.purpose: Create downloadable files from converted data

browser-api.name: URL.createObjectURL
browser-api.purpose: Generate download URLs for converted files

constraints.constraint: All processing must remain client-side (WASM) - no server uploads
Individual conversions must complete in less than 100ms per file
Must support Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+ (AbortController required)
Zero external JavaScript libraries - vanilla JS only
Sequential processing (one file at a time) to prevent memory pressure
Dropdown must show only valid target formats (exclude source format)
File status must persist across user interactions (queued, processing, complete, error)
Keyboard navigation required (Tab through controls, Enter to convert)

interface.name: convertFile (WASM Bridge)
interface.signature: async convertFile(data: Uint8Array, targetFormat: string, signal?: AbortSignal): Promise<{data: Uint8Array, format: string}>

interface.name: UploadManager.updateFileStatus
interface.signature: updateFileStatus(fileId: string, status: string, errorMessage?: string): void

tests.standards: Recipe uses manual browser testing for web UI stories (no automated JavaScript testing framework currently). Testing standards: Manual testing across browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge), device testing (Desktop 1920x1080, Tablet 768px, Mobile 375px), accessibility testing (keyboard navigation Tab/Enter/Esc, screen reader compatibility), performance validation (conversion timing less than 100ms target, UI responsiveness 60fps interactions), cross-browser feature detection (AbortController support check with graceful degradation)

locations.location: web/js/upload.js - Manual unit testing via browser console
web/css/components.css - Visual regression via manual inspection

test-case.title: Per-file format selection dropdown
test-case.steps: Upload NP3 file - Verify dropdown shows XMP, lrtemplate, Capture One, DCP (4 options, source excluded). Upload XMP file - Verify dropdown shows NP3, lrtemplate, Capture One, DCP. Verify dropdown disabled during processing state. Verify dropdown hidden when complete state.
test-case.expected: Format dropdown correctly excludes source format and shows all other valid formats alphabetically sorted

test-case.title: Individual Convert button
test-case.steps: Upload 3 files - Verify each has Convert button. Click Convert on file 2 - Verify only file 2 processes (files 1 and 3 remain queued). Verify button disabled during processing state. Verify button hidden when complete state (replaced by download).
test-case.expected: Individual file conversions work without affecting other files

test-case.title: Individual file conversion flow
test-case.steps: Upload 5 files - Select different formats for each. Convert file 3 first (not sequential) - Verify only file 3 processes. Convert file 1, then file 5 - Verify sequential processing works. Verify batch progress: 3 of 5 converted.
test-case.expected: Files can be converted in any order with accurate progress tracking

test-case.title: Mixed format conversion support
test-case.steps: Upload 3 files (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate). Select: NP3 to XMP, XMP to DCP, lrtemplate to NP3. Convert all - Verify each downloads with correct format. Verify filenames: preset_converted.xmp, style_converted.dcp, template_converted.np3.
test-case.expected: Each file converts to its custom selected format with correct filename extension

test-case.title: Retry failed conversions
test-case.steps: Upload corrupted file - Verify conversion fails - Retry button appears. Click Retry - Verify conversion re-attempts. Retry 5 times - Verify no limit on retry attempts. Verify error message clears when retry starts.
test-case.expected: Failed conversions can be retried indefinitely without limit

test-case.title: Batch vs individual conversion coexistence
test-case.steps: Upload 10 files - Batch convert 5 to XMP. Individually convert 3 to DCP - Verify both work together. Verify batch progress: 8 of 10 converted. Verify no conflict when triggering batch and individual simultaneously.
test-case.expected: Batch and individual conversions coordinate properly with shared progress tracking

test-case.title: Individual file cancellation
test-case.steps: Start conversion - Click Cancel during processing state. Verify status reverts to queued. Verify Convert button reappears. Verify cancelling file does not affect other files. Verify no confirmation dialog for individual cancel.
test-case.expected: Individual file conversions can be cancelled cleanly without affecting other files
